svg.title: Kotlin Android OOP Intermediate Roadmap
svg.desc: A 4-phase learning roadmap from OOP fundamentals to real-world Android patterns

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Phase 1: Class design in Kotlin with real Android examples')
g.text: Phase 1 — Class design that actually makes sense
Week 1–2
● When to use data class vs regular class vs object
● Sealed classes for state (loading / success / error)
● Constructor patterns, init blocks, companion objects

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Phase 2: Interfaces vs Abstract classes in Android with real examples')
g.text: Phase 2 — Interfaces vs abstract classes (the real difference)
Week 3–4
● Interface = what a class CAN do (contract)
● Abstract class = what a class IS (shared base)
● Real Android examples: Listeners, Adapters, BaseActivity

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Phase 3: Design patterns for Android Kotlin beginners')
g.text: Phase 3 — 3 design patterns every Android dev must know
Week 5–6
● Repository pattern — separating data from UI
● Observer pattern — LiveData, StateFlow, callbacks
● Singleton pattern — object keyword in Kotlin
● Factory pattern — creating objects cleanly

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Explain Phase 4: Writing clean real-world Android code with SOLID principles')
g.text: Phase 4 — Write clean, real-world Android code
Week 7–8
● SOLID principles — applied to Android, not textbooks
● ViewModel + Repository = MVVM architecture
● Dependency injection basics (manual DI before Hilt)
● Code review mindset — reading others' clean code

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Give me a mini Android project idea to practice all OOP concepts together')
g.text: Build 1 mini project per phase
Small Android apps that apply each concept — tap to get project ideas

svg.text: Tap any phase to dive deep into it
